threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look at the Tube map with hope instead of resignation, like it won’t derail your plans every other weekend.
Last night on the 35, a guy in a full crocodile onesie tried to convince everyone he was on a mission from 'the swamp.' Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When your neighbor borrowed your bike pump and returned it with a freshly baked Zopf. That's pure Neukölln hospitality, right there.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here will you find a bus driver who takes a coffee break mid-route, leaving passengers to chat about their plans. It's oddly charming.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local can spot a Mission burrito by the way someone holds it—like they're cradling a newborn, not just another meal.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy serenading the 14 bus stop crowd with a ukulele. Only in SF do you get spontaneous concerts while waiting for a ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every corner café serves great coffee. Local spots often hide in plain sight, pouring the best brews while the busy streets lure in the unsuspecting.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their fascination with the local birds. They stop mid-conversation, eyes wide, gushing over sparrows like they've never seen one before.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stare blankly at the bus stops, waiting for a timetable that doesn’t exist. Locals have their routes memorized like a secret map.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a guy tried to sell a can of Red Bull for a fiver. Must've thought we were tourists.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the lady in a polka dot dress arguing with a street musician over the volume of his accordion by the canal. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a place where they serve Currywurst on a ping pong table. It's like dinner and a game all rolled into one.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to seamlessly navigate around BART delays, while newcomers just stand there confused, checking their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that took a 15-minute detour just to avoid a street performer playing 'Wonderwall' on loop. Classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals go to the same bars, but most of us have our secret spots, tucked away from the tourist trails.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still awkwardly navigate the escalators at metro stops—holding tight to the rail and looking around confused.
